commit.message:
perf(kernels): vectorize softmax exp with AVX2+FMA polynomial (#3354)

## Summary
Replace scalar per-element exp() in both AVX2 softmax hot loops
(softmax.rs + simd_softmax.rs) with a fully vectorized Cephes-style
degree-5 minimax polynomial using FMA intrinsics. This eliminates the
store-to-buffer scalar-loop load-from-buffer anti-pattern that was
defeating SIMD throughput.

## What changed
**Both \softmax.rs\ and \simd_softmax.rs\:**
- **exp_avx2()**: New 8-wide vectorized exp(x) via 2^(xlog2e)
decomposition
  - Cody-Waite two-part ln2 range reduction (preserves precision)
  - Horner evaluation with \_mm256_fmadd_ps\ (5 FMA ops)
  - Bit-manipulation 2^n reconstruction (no libm dependency)
  - Max relative error < 2e-7 over [-88, 88]
- **softmax_avx2()** / **simd_softmax_avx2()**: Now call exp_avx2()
directly zero buffer bouncing

**softmax.rs only:**
- **softmax_avx2_inplace()**: New zero-allocation in-place variant
  - Eliminates data.to_vec() clone in \softmax_f32_inplace()\
- Reads and writes same buffer (each element read once then overwritten)

**All dispatch sites** now check both \vx2\ AND \ ma\ (all Haswell+ /
Kaby Lake CPUs have both)

## Receipt
- **what changed**: crates/bitnet-kernels/src/cpu/softmax.rs (+123/-15),
simd_softmax.rs (+52/-11)
- **what ran locally**:
- \cargo build -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu\
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- softmax\ 399 passed
- \cargo test -p bitnet-kernels --test avx2_scalar_parity_tests
--no-default-features --features cpu -- softmax\ 4 passed
- \cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --no-default-features --features
cpu -- -D warnings\ clean
- **CI relied on**: CI Core (Build & Test, Clippy, BDD Grid Check)
- **parity evidence**: All 4 AVX2-vs-scalar parity tests pass (aligned,
unaligned, stability, pipeline)
- **perf receipt**: No perf claim (structural improvement; benchmark in
follow-up)
- **docs touched**: none
- **remains**: Could add a targeted microbenchmark; vectorized exp could
also benefit layer_norm and attention
- **disposition**: MERGE (A)

## Why this matters
Softmax is called once per attention head per layer per token. For a
32-layer model with 32 heads, that's 1,024 softmax calls per token. The
old code bounced through a scalar buffer for every 8 elements, negating
AVX2 gains. This keeps all computation in vector registers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(apple-silicon): NEON-accelerated attention dot product (#3355)

Implement NEON SIMD fast-path for Q·K^T dot product in the attention
kernel, replacing scalar fallback on ARM64 targets.

## What Changed
- Added `neon_dot`: vectorized dot product using
`vld1q_f32`/`vfmaq_f32`/`vaddvq_f32`
- Added `neon_qk`/`neon_qk_into`: Q·K^T matrix computation using
`neon_dot`
- Updated `dispatch_qk`/`dispatch_qk_into` to auto-select NEON on
aarch64
- Uses baseline ARMv8-A NEON (always available, no runtime detection
needed)

## Expected Impact
~2-4× speedup for attention Q·K^T computation on Apple M-series silicon.

## Verification
- 760 attention tests pass ✅
- `dispatch_qk_matches_scalar` parity test confirms NEON matches
reference ✅
- `cargo clippy -p bitnet-kernels --lib --features cpu -- -D warnings` ✅

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Adds 8th implementation to the monorepo: Java 21 + Spring Boot 3.4.5

- **Gradle 8.14** (Kotlin DSL) + Spring Boot 3.4.5 + Spring Data JPA +
Hibernate 6 + Flyway

types + 3 tables

`"given offer"`, `"enterprise-software"`)

multi-criteria filtering with pagination

history capture

needed)

tests

## Frontend (`angular-spring-ui/`) — port 3070

- Angular 21 + Tailwind CSS 4.x following `angular-ui/` (spec 017)
patterns exactly
- Standalone components, Angular Signals, FormsModule,
`CanDeactivateFn<T>` unsaved-changes guard

## Monorepo Integration

- Root `package.json`: `dev/build/lint/test/migrate/install/audit:ci`
scripts for both packages + `:all` variants
- `playwright.config.ts`: port 3070 with `timeout: 120_000`
- `scripts/run-e2e.sh`: `spring` stack added; `scripts/stop-all.sh`:
ports 3070 + 8080
- CI: Java 21 (Temurin) + `gradle/actions/setup-gradle@v4`
- Docs: `README.md` (+ Java 21 prerequisite),
`docs/DATABASE_ARCHITECTURE.md`, `scripts/generate-schema-docs.sh`,
`CLAUDE.md`

## Also in this PR

- `api/` + `angular-ui/`: allowlist `GHSA-q5qw-h33p-qvwr` and
`GHSA-wc8c-qw6v-h7f6`; bump `cors` → 2.8.6, `prisma` → 7.4.2
- **CI fix**: committed missing `gradle-wrapper.jar` and fixed
`.gitignore` rule ordering (exception must come after wildcard)

- [x] `npm run build:spring-api` — Gradle build compiles cleanly
- [x] `npm run lint:spring-api` — Checkstyle passes
- [x] `npm run test:spring-api` — unit + `@WebMvcTest` tests pass
- [x] `npm run build:angular-spring-ui` — Angular build passes
- [x] `npm run lint:angular-spring-ui` — ESLint passes
- [x] `npm run test:angular-spring-ui` — Angular unit tests pass
- [x] Start both servers, verify all features at http://localhost:3070
- [x] `npm run test:e2e:spring` — 13 shared Playwright tests pass
- [x] `bash scripts/run-e2e.sh` — no regression across all stacks

commit.message:
perf: optimize editor cache and nav panel per-frame work

Performance:
- EditorGalleyCache: store only layout sections instead of full
  LayoutJob. On partial cache hits (resize/zoom), rebuild LayoutJob
  from document text + cached sections — eliminates 1x document-size
  clone on every partial hit. Steady-state memory reduced by 1x doc size.
- Nav panel: cache min_level in NavState, recompute only when outline
  or max_depth changes (was O(n) per frame).
- Nav panel: replace per-frame expanded_hash() O(n) scan with dirty
  generation counter — O(1) per frame.

Memory:
- Added type_size assertions: Span=12 bytes, SpanStyle=2 bytes.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

1. Type mismatch in handleVersionSelect: strict equality failed when
   the API returned version as a number but the dropdown provided a
   string. Added String() coercion for the comparison.

2. Stale preservedValue blocking serialization: dispatchParamUpdate
   updated the value segments but left preservedValue intact. The
   serializer checks preservedValue first, so it returned the old
   version number. Now clears preservedValue on every programmatic
   parameter update.

commit.message:
fix: robust sws color space handling with verification and fallback

The root cause of pink/green video artifacts: sws_getContext defaults to
srcRange=0 (limited-range 16-235), but GPU readback delivers full-range
RGB (0-255). Without srcRange=1, values outside 16-235 cause chroma
overflow in the YUV conversion, producing pink/green banding in decoders.

Changes:
- Check sws_setColorspaceDetails return value and log errors
- Verify settings took effect via sws_getColorspaceDetails
- If verification fails, fall back to sws_alloc_context + sws_init_context
  explicit initialization path with re-applied color space details
- Set YuvFrame color properties (range, primaries, trc, matrix) to match
  VUI metadata for belt-and-suspenders correctness
- Add frame 0 diagnostic: log center pixel BGRA→YCbCr conversion values
  and linesize for visual verification of the conversion pipeline

Refactor code structure for improved readability and data handling (#35)

* Refactor code structure for improved readability and maintainability

* Refactor data fetching: introduce fetchBundle method and mapBundleToMmmPayload for improved payload handling

* Update version to 0.7.3 in package.json and package-lock.json

* fix/refactor: remove duplicates, fix bugs, clarify naming

Bugs fixed:
- absences.js: nowYmd now respects ctx._currentTodayYmd so debugDate
  works correctly (was always using new Date() instead)
- dataFetchOrchestrator.js: fix 3 activeLogger calls with wrong
  signature (1 arg → text was passed as level, not message)

Renamed for clarity (no behaviour change):
- formatDateYYYYMMDD → formatDateISO in dateTimeUtils.js
  (was misleadingly named; returns YYYY-MM-DD, not YYYYMMDD)
- normalizeTimeToMinutes → normalizeTimeToHHMM in dataOrchestration.js
  and webuntisClient.js (returns HHMM integer, not minutes)
- sanitizeHtmlText → stripAllHtml in dataOrchestration.js
  (distinguishes it from sanitizeHtml which keeps a whitelist of tags)
- initWidget → resolveWidgetHelpers in util.js and all 6 widget files
  (function resolves helpers, does not initialise anything;
  initWidget kept as backward-compat alias)
- getFirstChangedName → getFirstFieldName in grid.js
  ("Changed" was misleading; returns current name of first array entry)
- studentCell → studentLabelText in absences/exams/homework/lessons.js
  (variable holds a string, not a DOM element)
- _fetchAppData: replace hidden arguments[3] with explicit options = {}
  parameter in authService.js

Duplicates removed:
- isIrregularStatus, getChangedFieldSet, getFirstFieldName were defined
  identically in both grid.js and lessons.js; moved to util.js and
  exported via resolveWidgetHelpers
- tryOrNull in errorUtils.js now delegates to tryOrDefault(fn, null)
  instead of duplicating the try/catch body
- Removed unused formatHolidayDate alias from util.js

Docs updated: ARCHITECTURE.md reflects renamed functions

* refactor: centralize parent authentication flow in _getParentAuthResult method

* refactor: rename time-related utility functions for clarity and introduce new parsing function

* refactor: extract common context retrieval into _extractRestRequestContext and simplify validation warnings collection

* refactor: streamline REST endpoint execution and authentication handling in WebUntisClient

* refactor: update time-related utility functions and remove legacy compatibility code

* refactor: introduce buildFetchFlags method and streamline widget fetch logic in WebUntisClient and node_helper

* refactor: streamline fetch logic by consolidating parameters and enhancing context handling in WebUntisClient and related modules

* refactor: enhance date range calculation by adopting a flat input structure and improving parameter handling in calculateFetchRanges

* refactor: enhance fetch logic by restructuring context handling and introducing a generic input shape for date range calculations

* refactor: enhance data fetching logic by introducing reusable fetch functions and improving response handling in WebUntis API service

* feat: Implement mmmPayloadMapper and payloadBuilder for structured data handling

- Added mmmPayloadMapper.js to map data bundles to MMM payloads.
- Introduced payloadBuilder.js to construct GOT_DATA payloads with compacting and warning management.
- Enhanced authService.js to improve logger injection for better flexibility.
- Updated dataFetchOrchestrator.js to include homework filter parameters for fetching homework data.
- Modified webuntisClient.js to integrate new payload mapping and homework filter logic.
- Adjusted node_helper.js to pass homework filter settings from student configuration.

* refactor: update architecture documentation to reflect timetable-first orchestration and improved fetch strategy

* refactor: update architecture documentation to reflect changes in function line numbers and service orchestration

Implement mirrored sprites and _ASMSet_Color_Xform in do_draw

Achieve full parity with sprite.cpp.decomp @ 0x004C01A0:
- Branch on get_facetindex return: mirrored!=0 calls shape_mirror,
  mirrored==0 calls shape_draw
- Add _ASMSet_Color_Xform((id+1)*0x10) calls before color table use
- Set SDI_Draw_Level and SDI_List->Select_Box per decomp
- Remove old TODO about missing mirror/xform parity

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
